scenarios.name: missing_display_rank
scenarios.action: block IPS correction and fail corpus validation
scenarios.interview_point: Position-bias correction is impossible without logged display rank.

scenarios.name: invalid_display_rank_range
scenarios.interview_point: Ranking logs need strict position semantics before evaluation.

scenarios.name: low_candidate_recall
scenarios.trigger: hybrid candidate recall drops below popularity baseline by large margin
scenarios.action: fallback to popularity + content hybrid and mark retrieval quality degraded
scenarios.interview_point: Items not retrieved cannot be ranked; retrieval recall is a first-order gate.

scenarios.name: temporal_split_not_used
scenarios.trigger: ranking baseline evaluated with random split
scenarios.action: reject evaluation and require temporal holdout rerun
scenarios.interview_point: Random split can leak future popularity and behavior into ranking evaluation.

scenarios.name: propensity_missing_or_zero
scenarios.trigger: rank-level click propensity missing or zero
scenarios.action: apply propensity floor and rerun IPS validation
scenarios.interview_point: IPS can explode when propensities are too small; clipping controls variance.

scenarios.name: ips_variance_explosion
scenarios.trigger: IPS weight distribution has extreme max weight
scenarios.action: increase propensity floor, report limitation, and mark estimate high-variance
scenarios.interview_point: Debiasing improves validity but can increase variance.

scenarios.name: seller_exposure_concentration
scenarios.action: hold promotion and rerun governed reranker with stricter seller cap
scenarios.interview_point: Marketplace ranking is not just relevance; supply-side exposure matters.

scenarios.name: category_monoculture
scenarios.action: apply MMR/category constraint and monitor relevance tradeoff
scenarios.interview_point: Diversity constraints are a deliberate relevance-vs-marketplace-health tradeoff.

scenarios.name: delayed_attribution_window_missing
scenarios.action: block A/B simulation until delayed attribution is generated
scenarios.interview_point: CTR alone is insufficient; purchases arrive after impression time.

scenarios.name: return_adjustment_missing
scenarios.action: block primary metric readout and regenerate return-adjusted labels
scenarios.interview_point: Revenue/session should be adjusted for returns in marketplace evaluation.

scenarios.name: guardrail_breach_despite_primary_lift
scenarios.trigger: treatment improves net RPS but violates seller/category/return guardrail
scenarios.interview_point: A positive primary metric is not enough to ship if guardrails fail.

scenarios.name: metasignal_export_schema_mismatch
scenarios.trigger: PulseRank event export missing metric_name or unit_of_analysis
scenarios.action: block integration export and regenerate schema-compatible events
scenarios.interview_point: Portfolio integration should be schema-compatible, not hand-wavy.

scenarios.name: cold_start_items_starved
scenarios.trigger: new or sparse-history items receive no exposure
scenarios.detection_signal: cold_start_events count below expected and catalog coverage low
scenarios.action: trigger content-based fallback and exploration allocation
scenarios.interview_point: Ranking systems need cold-start fallback, not only popularity loops.

scenarios.name: exploration_over_allocation
scenarios.action: throttle exploration and prioritize deterministic ranking
scenarios.interview_point: Exploration is useful but must be bounded by policy.

scenarios.name: offline_ab_overclaim
scenarios.trigger: offline simulation interpreted as real online experiment
scenarios.detection_signal: claim boundary check detects online A/B claim
scenarios.action: rewrite report to clarify offline simulation only
scenarios.interview_point: Truth boundary is part of the project quality, not a weakness.

evidence_statement: Defines failure and recovery scenarios for PulseRank ranking-system reliability, evaluation validity, guardrail behavior, integration safety, and claim-boundary enforcement.
